provenance.title: GeomHerd: A Forward-looking Herding Quantification via Ricci Flow Geometry on Agent Interactive Simulations

authors.name: Junwei Su
authors.affiliation: University of Science and Technology of China

provenance.abstract_truncated: GeomHerd is a forward-looking geometric framework for herding quantification. By tracking Ollivier-Ricci curvature of agent-action graphs in LLM-driven multi-agent financial simulators, it anticipates herding earlier than price-correlation or trading-flow baselines, with median lead of 272 steps before order-parameter onset.
key_claims: GeomHerd fires a median 272 steps before order-parameter herding onset on the CWS substrate (recall-oriented operating point)
Contagion detector beta_minus recalls 65% of critical trajectories with 318-step lead
Agent-graph curvature signal precedes price-correlation-graph baselines by 40 steps on co-firing trajectories
Mean-field bridge to CSAD established: CSAD_t = sigma_xi * sqrt(2/pi) * (1 - kappa_OR)^(1/2) + O(N^{-1/2})
Curvature transfers out-of-domain to Vicsek particle model with AUROC 0.99; GeomHerd-conditioned forecasting head reduces cascade-window log-return MAE

architecture.description: A four-stage geometric pipeline operating on dynamic agent-action graphs. Components: (1) graph construction, (2) Ollivier-Ricci curvature computation via LP-Wasserstein, (3) sign-decomposed CUSUM/Kendall-tau detection, (4) forward-looking Ricci-flow singularity time and effective vocabulary.

components.name: AgentGraphConstructor
components.inputs: Action sequences a_i(t) in {buy, hold, sell} for N agents over T steps
components.outputs: Weighted sparse graph G_t = (V, E_t, w_t), |V|=N=66
components.operation: Windowed agreement frequency w_t(i,j) = (1/Tw) * sum_{s=t-Tw+1}^{t} 1[a_i(s)=a_j(s)]; sparsify at w0=0.5; reconstruct every dt=10 steps with Tw=100

components.name: OllivierRicciCurvatureEngine
components.outputs: Per-edge curvature kappa_OR(i,j;t) in R; mean curvature kappa_bar_OR(t); sign decomposition (kappa_bar_plus, beta_minus)
components.operation: Lazy-walk kernel mu_i(j) = alpha*delta_ij + (1-alpha)*w_t(i,j)/sum_k(w_t(i,k)); alpha=0.5. Edge distance d(i,j)=w_t(i,j). kappa_OR(i,j;t) = 1 - W1(mu_i, mu_j)/d(i,j). W1 solved via LP (POT library). Sign split: E+ if kappa>0.1, E- if kappa<-0.1

components.name: CUSUMDetector
components.operation: S_plus_t = max(0, S_plus_{t-1} + (kappa_bar_plus_OR(t) - mu_plus_base - k_plus)); A_plus_t = 1[S_plus_t > h_plus]. Contagion: S_minus_t = max(0, S_minus_{t-1} + (beta_minus(t) - mu_minus_base - k_minus)); A_minus_CUSUM_t = 1[S_minus_t > h_minus]; A_minus_t = A_minus_CUSUM_t OR 1[Kendall_{[t-W_tau,t]}(beta_minus) > tau_thresh]

components.name: RicciFlowSingularityDescriptor
components.outputs: tau_sing(t): first neckpinch time under discrete Ricci flow starting from G_t
components.operation: At every snapshot, run a fresh discrete Ricci flow on G_t. Record tau_sing(t) = inf{s>0 : exists e in E_t, kappa_OR^(s)(e) -> -inf}. Produces a forward-looking time-to-coordination series.

components.name: EffectiveVocabularyTracker
components.inputs: Agent action distribution at time t
components.operation: Fixed 3D FSQ codebook with Ld=4 levels per dimension => K=64 codewords total (non-learned). H(p_t) is entropy of codebook utilization distribution. V_eff contracts as herding develops.

components.name: KronosForecasting Head
components.inputs: GeomHerd triplet (kappa_bar_OR, tau_sing, V_eff); OHLCV sequences via frozen price tokeniser
components.operation: Frozen learned vector-quantiser tokenises OHLCV -> token vocabulary. Transformer conditioned on GeomHerd triplet via AdaLN-Zero (adaptive layer-norm, zero-initialised gating). Only conditioning layers trained.

Vicsek.name: Vicsek self-driven particle model
Vicsek.agent_graph: k-NN (k=10) on heading sequence, binary edge weights

ambiguities.description: Discrete Ricci flow iteration rule not fully specified — only the stopping criterion (neckpinch) is given
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Standard Hamilton-type discrete Ricci flow: w_{t+1}(e) = w_t(e) - kappa_OR(e;t) * w_t(e) * step_size

ambiguities.description: tau_thresh and W_tau values deferred to Appendix D which gives only the CUSUM sweep, not explicit Kendall values
ambiguities.primary_assumption: tau_thresh=-0.4 (inferred from Table 3 beta_minus row label)

ambiguities.description: Architecture of Kronos-style transformer (number of layers, heads, hidden dim) not specified
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Small transformer: 2-4 layers, 4-8 heads, d_model=64 or 128 (typical for financial tabular tasks)

equations.name: Windowed action-agreement edge weight
equations.role: Graph construction — edge weight between agents i and j

equations.name: Lazy-walk transition kernel
equations.role: Node measure for Wasserstein transport in Ricci curvature computation; alpha=0.5

equations.name: CUSUM statistic for contagion (beta_minus)
equations.role: CUSUM component of contagion bridge detector

equations.name: Combined contagion alarm
equations.role: OR-combination of CUSUM level and Kendall-tau trend for bridge detection

equations.name: Mean-field bridge to CSAD (Proposition 1)
equations.role: Theoretical link between agent-graph curvature and classical CSAD herding measure
equations.note: Stated as dominant-order scaling identity; full rigorous proof deferred to follow-on work

equations.name: Augmented CCK regression
equations.role: Empirical validation: gamma_3 < 0 confirms sign-consistency with Proposition 1

equations.name: Effective vocabulary
equations.role: Behavioral homogenisation signal; H(p_t) is entropy of FSQ codebook utilization distribution

tensors.name: action_sequence
tensors.notes: N=66 agents, T=simulation steps (up to ~1200 in CWS traces)

tensors.name: edge_weight_matrix
tensors.notes: Sparse after sparsification at w0=0.5; reconstructed every dt=10 steps

tensors.name: lazy_walk_kernel
tensors.notes: Row-stochastic after alpha=0.5 laziness; passed pairwise to W1 solver

tensors.name: per_edge_curvature
tensors.notes: One scalar per retained edge; sign-decomposed into E+, E0, E-

tensors.name: curvature_time_series
tensors.notes: kappa_bar_plus_OR(t) and beta_minus(t); subsampled at stride delta_t=10

tensors.name: CUSUM_statistic
tensors.notes: S_plus_t and S_minus_t maintained as running scalars

tensors.name: geomherd_triplet
tensors.notes: (kappa_bar_OR, tau_sing, V_eff) used as conditioning for Kronos head via AdaLN-Zero

training_pipeline.note: GeomHerd is primarily an inference/detection pipeline, not a trained model. The only trained component is the Kronos forecasting head.

geometric_pipeline.note: No training required; pure algorithmic pipeline

kronos_forecasting_head.loss: Log-return MAE (mean absolute error on cascade window)
kronos_forecasting_head.price_tokeniser: Frozen learned vector-quantiser on OHLCV; trained separately
kronos_forecasting_head.note: Architecture details (layers, heads, hidden dim) not specified in paper

cusum_calibration.method: Sweep over (k_sigma, h_sigma) grid on binary-edge replay set (400 trajectories)
cusum_calibration.baseline_window: W=35 samples of subsampled curvature trace (W*delta_t = 350 simulator steps)

datasets.name: CWS replay set
datasets.splits: No train/test split noted for detection; Kronos head training split not specified
datasets.generation: 80 seeds x 5 coupling levels kappa in {0.5,0.8,1.2,1.8,2.5}

datasets.name: Vicsek transfer set
datasets.size: 100 trajectories (20 seeds x 5 noise levels eta in {0.5,1.0,1.6,2.0,2.5}), 95 valid

metrics: Conditional median lead time (steps) with 95% bootstrap CI

baselines.name: AA-MI (Tessera 2026)
baselines.note: Degenerate output on all 400 trajectories under binary-edge configuration

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Discrete Ricci flow update rule: w_{t+1}(e) = w_t(e) * (1 - step_size * kappa_OR(e;t))
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard formulation in curvature-based graph rewiring literature; Lin-Lu-Yau or Ollivier variant

implementation_assumptions.assumption: LLM agent simulator: each of N=66 agents is a separate persona-conditioned call to Bedrock Claude Opus 4.6 (stated in paper body for headline runs); for reproduction, a smaller Claude model or open-source LLM may substitute without affecting the geometric pipeline
implementation_assumptions.basis: Explicitly stated in paper for CWS experiments

implementation_assumptions.assumption: FSQ codebook: 3D, 4 levels per dim, K=64 codewords; non-learned; agents' actions projected into this space for entropy computation
POT library (Python Optimal Transport) for exact LP Wasserstein-1 computation
implementation_assumptions.basis: Explicitly stated in Section 2.2 and Appendix C

implementation_assumptions.assumption: SciPy CSR-graph Dijkstra for all-pairs shortest paths (stated in Appendix C)
Kronos forecasting head architecture: small transformer ~2-4 layers with AdaLN-Zero conditioning; price tokeniser is a VQ-VAE on OHLCV sequences
implementation_assumptions.basis: Inferred from 'Kronos-style' reference and AdaLN-Zero mention; no layer/head counts given

implementation_assumptions.assumption: HAC standard errors for CCK regression use Newey-West with automatic bandwidth selection
implementation_assumptions.basis: Stated as 'Newey-West' in Section 3.3.2; bandwidth not specified

implementation_assumptions.assumption: CWS substrate dynamics: each agent's action drawn from a distribution driven by private signal + neighbor-average weighted by coupling kappa + idiosyncratic Gaussian noise; linear price impact rule
implementation_assumptions.basis: Described in Section 3.2 and referenced to Cividino et al. 2023
alternatives: Refer to original CWS paper for full mechanics

ambiguities.description: Discrete Ricci flow iteration rule (how curvature drives edge weight update) is not written out; only stopping criterion (neckpinch) is given
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Standard multiplicative update: w(e) <- w(e) - eta * kappa(e) * w(e)

ambiguities.description: Kendall-tau threshold tau_thresh and window W_tau are not given numerically in the main text or Appendix D table
Kronos forecasting head architecture (layers, hidden dim, heads) not specified
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Small transformer with AdaLN-Zero conditioning, frozen VQ-VAE price tokeniser

ambiguities.description: LLM persona prompts intentionally withheld by authors; reproduction requires designing persona-conditioned prompts
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Generic risk-appetite / momentum / herding-tendency persona prompts can approximate the paper's setup
